hypothesis: Replacing the token-level cross-entropy training objective with a hybrid loss that combines cross-entropy with a differentiable, spectrum-derived ΔE penalty computed via the transfer-matrix method will reduce the generalization gap between training-paraphrase and held-out validation performance (currently ΔE ≈1 vs. ≈34) by at least 40%, because directly optimizing the decoder against the perceptual color metric will steer sequence generation away from physically plausible but spectrally mismatched layer orderings that cross-entropy alone cannot penalize.
